# Cross-repo dependency-loop simulation â€” gap report

**Generated by:** `bash scripts/herd/sim/cross-repo-loop-sim.sh`
**Foundation:** `.herd/links` cross-repo link registry (PR #14)
**Scope:** the full Aâ†’B loop â€” consumer files issue â†’ provider ships â†’ consumer detects-done â†’ `herd upgrade` â†’ consumer unblocks

This document records which primitives the simulation had to stub out, exactly what each stub
replaced, and which deferred backlog items own the real work. It doubles as the **Phase-4
brownfield test specification**: when the primitives below are built, this file describes the
acceptance criteria.

---

## What is REAL (confirmed working)

| Step | Primitive | Shipped in |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| 1 | `herd link list` â€” resolve a named peer from `.herd/links` | PR #14 |
| 2 | `herd report --to <link>` â€” file an issue on a peer repo via `_backend_add_item` | PR #10, #14 |
| 4 | provider ship signal â€” provider's normal coordinator/scribe close the issue on PR merge | PR #25 (Gap 5 resolved by Gap 1 + Gap 2) |
| 5 | `_backend_item_state <ref>` â€” 4th adapter op in all four backends | PR #25 |
| 6 | dep-watcher singleton â€” `scripts/herd/dep-watcher.sh` with spawn-lock + backoff | PR #25 |
| 7 | `herd upgrade` â€” re-render the coordinator skill from the current engine template | CLI (bin/herd) |

The link registry (`herd-links.sh: _herd_resolve_link`) correctly wires `HERD_REPO` and
`HERD_REPORT_BACKEND` for the target peer. The dedup-before-filing logic (`_report_find_dups`)
is also confirmed working at step 2.

---

## âś… Gap 1 â€” `_backend_item_state` (4th adapter op) â€” SHIPPED (PR #25)

**Sim step:** 5  
**Backlog item:** `_backend_item_state <id> op + dependency-watcher`  
**What the stub did:** shelled out to `gh issue view --json state` and returned OPEN or CLOSED.

### What was built

A fourth op alongside `add_item / mark_shipped / list_open` in every backend adapter:

```
_backend_item_state <ref>   â†’ sets ITEM_STATE=open|closed|in-progress
```

**Per-backend implementation:**

- `backends/github.sh` â€” `gh issue view <n> -R "$HERD_REPO" --json state` â†’ maps OPENâ†’open, CLOSEDâ†’closed
- `backends/linear.sh` â€” GraphQL `issueSearch` â†’ `state.type` â†’ maps completed/cancelledâ†’closed, startedâ†’in-progress, othersâ†’open
- `backends/file.sh` â€” greps `$BACKLOG_FILE` for the item slug; checks đź”ś/đźš§/âś… emoji â†’ open/in-progress/closed
- `backends/changelog.sh` â€” checks `[Unreleased]` section; slug present = `open`; absent = `closed`

**Input format:** `<link-name>#<id>` (e.g. `provider-lib#42`). The caller resolves the link
to `HERD_REPO` via `_herd_resolve_link` before calling this op.

---

## âś… Gap 2 â€” Dependency watcher â€” SHIPPED (PR #25)

**Sim step:** 6  
**Backlog item:** `_backend_item_state <id> op + dependency-watcher`  
**What the stub did:** one synchronous poll cycle â€” called `_backend_item_state_stub` and printed the state.

### What was built

`scripts/herd/dep-watcher.sh` â€” a persistent per-project background process (singleton):

1. Reads `.herd/deps` to discover all `blocked-on:` entries for this project.
2. Polls each dep's `_backend_item_state` on an interval with exponential backoff.
3. When a dep transitions to `closed`, removes the entry from `.herd/deps` and fires a herdr notification.
4. Surfaces `stalled` deps (open > TTL) as warnings rather than silent rot.

**Singleton pattern:** follows the `agent-watch.sh` spawn-lock (PR #21):
flock(1) when available, atomic-mkdir PID-file otherwise; per-project lock key from
`HERD_DEPWATCHER_LOCK` (added to `herd-config.sh`).

**Env knobs:** `DEP_POLL_MIN` (default 30s), `DEP_POLL_MAX` (default 300s), `DEP_STALE_TTL` (default 86400s).

---

## âś… Gap 3 â€” Blocked-on state: `.herd/deps` schema and `herd depend` command â€” SHIPPED (PR #90, HERD-385)

**Sim steps:** 3, 8  
**Backlog item:** `Dispatch vs. dependency intent`  
**What the stub did:** appended `blocked-on: <ref>` to `.herd/deps`; removed it with `grep -v`.

### What was built

**`.herd/deps` file format** (`bin/herd: _deps_ensure_file`):

```
# .herd/deps â€” recorded cross-repo dependencies (herd deps). Commit this; zero-secret.
# Format: blocked-on: <link>#<id>  since=<epoch>   â€” watched by dep-watcher.sh
#         watch: <link>#<id>  since=<epoch>        â€” demoted, non-blocking (ignored by the watcher)
blocked-on: provider-lib#42  since=1751000000
```

The row is byte-compatible with the parser `dep-watcher.sh` already shipped in Gap 2 â€” `ref` is
everything up to the first whitespace, so the trailing ` since=<epoch>` field is invisible to it.

- **`herd depend <link>#<id>`** â€” records a `blocked-on:` row (resolves the peer via
  `.herd/links` first), stamped with `since=`. Re-running it on a demoted `watch:` row promotes
  the row back to `blocked-on:` â€” a dep is never stuck.
- **`herd deps list`** â€” shows every recorded dep (both `blocked-on` and `watch` kinds) with its
  live upstream state via `_backend_item_state`.
- **`herd deps rm <link>#<id>`** â€” removes the row outright; warns (does not block) if the
  upstream is still open.
- **`herd deps demote <link>#<id>`** â€” reclassifies a `blocked-on:` row into a non-blocking
  `watch:` row, invisible to the watcher's parser, so the lane un-blocks instantly while the item
  stays on the radar.
- **`herd report --to <link> --dep`** â€” files the issue on the peer AND records the resulting
  item as a dep in one step (recovers the id from the backend's create result). The bare
  `--to` form (no `--dep`) stays fire-and-forget, recording no dep.

**Distinction from `herd report --to`:** `herd report` is fire-and-forget (dispatch intent);
`herd depend` is a watched dependency (tracked, blocks a lane). Same link registry; different
lifecycle.

Capability rows in `templates/capabilities.tsv` (`herd depend`, `herd deps`, `herd report --dep`,
`.herd/deps`) and conformance proof in `templates/conformance.tsv`
(`tests/test-dispatch-deps.sh`). Hermetic test asserts the add/list/rm/demote/promote round-trip,
ref-validation refusals, and that the recorded row is accepted by `dep-watcher.sh`'s own
`_dw_remove_dep` â€” proving byte-compatibility against the real consumer, not just a
self-consistent shape.

---

## Gap 4 â€” `herd upgrade` versioned migrations

**Sim step:** 7b  
**Backlog item:** `herd upgrade versioned migrations`  
**What the stub does:** nothing â€” `herd upgrade` already re-renders the coordinator skill
(REAL), but the migration scripts it would call do not exist.

### What needs to be built

A `migrations/` directory at the herdkit repo root:

```
migrations/
  v1â†’v2.sh    # transform .herd/config and .claude/commands/ for the v2 engine contract
  v2â†’v3.sh
  ...
```

**Each migration script interface:**

```
migrations/vNâ†’vM.sh <project-root>
  # reads $PROJECT_ROOT/.herd/config
  # rewrites config keys / renames hooks / adjusts coordinator skill as needed
  # exits 0 on success; 1 on unresolvable conflict (escalates to human)
```

`herd upgrade` would run the chain of applicable migrations in order, then re-render the
coordinator skill. A project at v1 upgrading to v3 runs v1â†’v2 then v2â†’v3.

**Why migrations matter in the cross-repo loop:** if the PROVIDER shipped a breaking engine
change (e.g. a new `.herd/config` key becomes required), the CONSUMER's `herd upgrade` needs
to run the right migration â€” not just re-render the old template.

---

## âś… Gap 5 â€” Provider ship signal / detection â€” RESOLVED (no new primitive)

**Sim step:** 4  
**Resolved by:** implementing Gap 1 + Gap 2.

The sim reveals that "provider ships" means the provider's OWN coordinator/scribe run normally
(closing the GitHub issue when the PR merges). The consumer's dep-watcher then detects the
closure via `_backend_item_state`. No special "ship signal" primitive is required on the
PROVIDER side.

---

## Phase-4 brownfield test protocol

When the remaining primitives are implemented, run this sequence to verify the real loop:

1. In a consumer project, add a `.herd/links` entry pointing at a real provider repo.
2. `herd report --to <provider> "blocked on X"` â€” verify issue created on provider. âś… REAL
3. `herd depend <provider>#<issue>` â€” verify `.herd/deps` updated. âś… REAL (Gap 3). Note: this only
   records the row; it does not itself spawn `dep-watcher.sh` (a separate long-running process).
4. `herd deps list` â€” verify dep shows OPEN state. âś… REAL (Gap 3)
5. On the provider side, close the issue (PR merge or manual close). âś… REAL
6. `herd deps list` again â€” verify watcher detected CLOSED. âś… REAL (dep-watcher.sh)
7. `herd deps rm <provider>#<issue>` â€” verify clean removal. âś… REAL (Gap 3)
8. `herd upgrade` â€” verify coordinator skill re-rendered; any applicable migration ran. (Gap 4)

**Regression check:** run `bash scripts/herd/sim/cross-repo-loop-sim.sh` and confirm all
steps still pass. The sim's own steps 1â€“8 are now [REAL] (steps 3 and 8 call the real
`herd depend` / `herd deps rm` as of this PR); only its step 7b (Gap 4 migrations) remains
[STUB].
